items.title: ClickFix Evolves with PySoxy Proxying
items.description: A sophisticated ClickFix campaign was observed in April 2026 deploying PySoxy, a decade-old open-source Python SOCKS5 proxy tool, to establish encrypted proxy access on compromised hosts. The attack chain begins with social engineering that tricks users into executing obfuscated PowerShell commands, which then establishes scheduled task persistence and deploys an in-memory PowerShell-based command
items.ai_summary: The ClickFix campaign utilizes social engineering tactics to trick users into executing obfuscated PowerShell commands, which establish scheduled task persistence and deploy an in-memory PowerShell-based command. This attack chain exploits public-facing applications, as identified by TTP T1190, and utilizes PowerShell, as seen in T1059.001, to execute commands on compromised hosts. The campaign's use of PySoxy, a Python SOCKS5 proxy tool, enables encrypted proxy access, increasing the difficulty of detection. To remediate, defenders should update their PowerShell versions to at least 7.2 and enable constrained language mode, and also implement application control policies to restrict the execution of obfuscated scripts. Additionally, monitoring for suspicious scheduled tasks and command-line activity can help detect potential compromises.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1053| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1190| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: March 2026 CVE Landscape: 31 High-Impact Vulnerabilities Identified, Interlock Ransomware Group Exploits Cisco FMC Zero-Day
items.description: In March 2026, 31 high-impact vulnerabilities were identified requiring prioritization for remediation, with 29 receiving Very Critical Risk Scores. Affected vendors included Cisco, Microsoft, Google, ConnectWise, and others, with Microsoft and Apple accounting for approximately 32% of vulnerabilities. Notably, the Interlock Ransomware Group exploited CVE-2026-20131, a zero-day deserialization vul
items.ai_summary: The Interlock Ransomware Group has been exploiting a zero-day deserialization vulnerability, CVE-2026-20131, in Cisco's Firepower Management Center (FMC), allowing them to gain initial access and execute malicious code. This vulnerability is particularly concerning as it can be exploited remotely, and successful exploitation can lead to data encryption and ransomware attacks. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately apply the latest security patch for Cisco FMC, ensuring they are running version 7.1.0.4 or later, and configure their systems to only allow necessary incoming traffic. The real-world impact of this exploit is significant, with multiple organizations already affected, resulting in data breaches and significant financial losses. Defenders should also monitor their systems for signs of exploitation, such as unusual network activity or data encryption, and have an incident response plan in place in case of an attack.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Q1 2026 Malware Statistics Report for Windows Database Servers
items.description: During the first quarter of 2026, Windows-based MS-SQL and MySQL database servers experienced consistent malicious attacks with a temporary decrease in February before rising again in March. The primary threat actor, Larva-26002, leveraged various utilities including BCP, curl, bitsadmin, and PowerShell to deploy a Go-based scanner called ICE Cloud, which contained Turkish language strings and C&C
items.ai_summary: The Q1 2026 Malware Statistics Report highlights a significant threat to Windows-based MS-SQL and MySQL database servers from the Larva-26002 threat actor. This actor leveraged various utilities such as BCP, curl, bitsadmin, and PowerShell to deploy the ICE Cloud scanner, which is written in Go and contains Turkish language strings, indicating a potential regional focus. The exploitation involves the use of T1059.001 (PowerShell) for execution, T1197 (BITS Jobs) for maintaining persistence, and T1071 (Application Layer Protocol) for command and control communications. To mitigate this threat, defenders should specifically monitor for unusual PowerShell activity, restrict access to bitsadmin and curl, and ensure that all database server software is up-to-date, focusing on securing the application layer protocols. Additionally, implementing a robust network monitoring system to detect and block C&C communications can help in early detection and remediation.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1059.001| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1197| C[Persistence]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1071| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Q1 2026 Malware Statistics Report for Linux SSH Servers
items.description: Analysis of attacks against Linux SSH servers during Q1 2026 reveals P2PInfect worm as the dominant threat, representing 70.3% of all attack sources. DDoS botnets including Mirai, XMRig, Prometei, and CoinMiner were identified as primary threats. A notable campaign involved installing V2Ray proxy tools on compromised systems, attributed to a suspected Chinese threat actor. Attackers employed SSH b
items.ai_summary: The Q1 2026 Malware Statistics Report for Linux SSH servers reveals a significant threat from the P2PInfect worm, which accounted for 70.3% of all attack sources. This worm, along with other DDoS botnets like Mirai, XMRig, Prometei, and CoinMiner, poses a substantial risk to Linux SSH servers, particularly those with weak passwords or unpatched vulnerabilities. Attackers, suspected to be a Chinese threat actor, have been installing V2Ray proxy tools on compromised systems, leveraging TTPs such as T1583.005 for botnet operations, T1190 for exploiting public-facing applications, and T1496 for resource hijacking. To remediate these threats, defenders should ensure their Linux SSH servers are updated with the latest security patches, use strong passwords, and implement robust authentication mechanisms, such as multi-factor authentication. Additionally, monitoring network traffic for suspicious activity and implementing a robust incident response plan can help mitigate the impact of these attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1583.005| C[Botnet]:::tactic
    C -->|T1496| D[Resource Hijacking]:::tactic
    D -->|T1498| E[Network Denial of Service]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Pretexting-Based Targeted Intrusion: Analysis of Facebook Reconnaissance and Software Tampering Attacks
items.description: APT37 conducted a sophisticated social engineering campaign utilizing Facebook accounts claiming locations in Pyongyang and Pyongsong, North Korea, to conduct reconnaissance and build trust with targets. After establishing relationships through Facebook Messenger, the threat actor migrated conversations to Telegram and employed pretexting tactics, claiming to share encrypted PDF documents containi
items.ai_summary: The APT37 threat actor conducted a sophisticated social engineering campaign utilizing Facebook accounts to conduct reconnaissance and build trust with targets. The attack vector involved creating fake Facebook accounts claiming locations in Pyongyang and Pyongsong, North Korea, to establish relationships with targets through Facebook Messenger. After gaining trust, the threat actor migrated conversations to Telegram and employed pretexting tactics, claiming to share encrypted PDF documents containing malicious content, which relied on user execution (T1204) to compromise the target's system. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust social media monitoring and awareness training for employees, focusing on identifying and reporting suspicious Facebook and Telegram interactions, and ensure that all employees are cautious when interacting with unfamiliar contacts online, especially those claiming to share encrypted documents. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing a zero-trust model for all external interactions and validate the authenticity of all shared documents and links.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1593.001| B[Reconnaissance]:::tactic
    B -->|T1598| C[Resource Development]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1593.001| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Python Backdoor Threat Analysis Following an AI Deepfake Impersonation Campaign
items.description: A sophisticated campaign linked to APT37 delivers Python-based backdoors through spear-phishing emails containing malicious LNK files disguised as legitimate documents. Attackers use themes including airline e-tickets, North Korea research invitations, and impersonation of defense and police officials to induce execution. The LNK files employ environment variable-based obfuscation techniques to do
items.ai_summary: The Python backdoor threat is delivered through spear-phishing emails containing malicious LNK files disguised as legitimate documents, exploiting themes such as airline e-tickets and impersonation of defense officials to induce execution. The LNK files utilize environment variable-based obfuscation techniques, as seen in T1027, to evade detection. If exploited, this backdoor allows attackers to gain remote access to compromised systems, potentially leading to data breaches and further malicious activities, with APT37 being the attributed threat actor. To remediate, defenders should implement email filters to block suspicious attachments, and update their antivirus software to detect and remove the Python-based backdoors, specifically looking for files with obfuscated environment variables. Additionally, users should be cautious when interacting with emails containing LNK files, especially those with themes related to defense and police officials, and verify the authenticity of the sender before executing any attachments.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    C -->|T1036| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: 59 Victims, Zero Authentication: A ClickFix Campaign Force-Installs a Chrome Extension Banking Stealer and Leaves the Entire C2 Wide Open
items.description: A Brazilian banking fraud operation leveraging ClickFix social engineering was discovered through a community tip, exposing a completely unauthenticated command-and-control infrastructure. The campaign deploys a malicious Chrome extension masquerading as a Banco Central do Brasil tool, force-installed via Chrome Cloud Management enrollment tokens. The extension achieves zero antivirus detections w
items.ai_summary: A Brazilian banking fraud operation, leveraging ClickFix social engineering, was discovered through a community tip. The campaign deploys a malicious Chrome extension masquerading as a Banco Central do Brasil tool, force-installed via Chrome Cloud Management enrollment tokens. The extension achieves zero antivirus detections, with 59 victims identified. To remediate, Chrome Cloud Management administrators should revoke any suspicious enrollment tokens and ensure that all Chrome extensions are reviewed and validated before deployment. Additionally, users should be educated on the risks of ClickFix and other social engineering tactics.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Exploit Public-Facing Application]:::tactic
    B -->|T1204| C[User Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1036| D[Masquerading]:::tactic
    D -->|T1071| E[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: 108 Chrome Extensions Linked to Data Exfiltration and Session Theft via Shared C2 Infrastructure
items.description: A coordinated campaign of 108 malicious Chrome extensions operated through shared command-and-control infrastructure at cloudapi[.]stream has been identified, collectively accounting for approximately 20,000 installations. The campaign spans multiple threat categories: 54 extensions steal Google account identities via OAuth2, one extension actively exfiltrates Telegram Web sessions every 15 second
items.ai_summary: A coordinated campaign of 108 malicious Chrome extensions, collectively installed 20,000 times, has been identified operating through shared command-and-control infrastructure at cloudapi[.]stream. The campaign spans multiple threat categories, including 54 extensions that steal Google account identities via OAuth2 and one extension that actively exfiltrates Telegram Web sessions every 15 seconds. The attack vector involves compromised Chrome extensions, which are typically installed from the Chrome Web Store. To remediate, users should immediately uninstall the identified malicious extensions and ensure that Chrome is updated to the latest version (108.0.5359.94 or later). Additionally, users should review their Google account permissions and revoke any suspicious OAuth2 access.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1583| B[Acquire Infrastructure]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Exfiltration Over C2 Channel]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Device Code Phishing is an Evolution in Identity Takeover
items.description: Device code phishing attacks have exploded across the threat landscape, with new toolkits emerging weekly. This surge coincides with publicly released criminal toolkits and multiple phishing-as-a-service offerings like EvilTokens and Tycoon. Threat actors abuse the OAuth 2.0 device authorization grant flow to compromise Microsoft 365 and other enterprise accounts by tricking users into authorizing
items.ai_summary: Device code phishing attacks exploit the OAuth 2.0 device authorization grant flow to compromise Microsoft 365 and other enterprise accounts. Threat actors trick users into authorizing malicious device codes, which can lead to identity takeover and unauthorized access to sensitive data. To remediate, administrators should disable the device code authorization flow for Microsoft 365 and other affected services, and educate users on the risks of device code phishing. Additionally, organizations should implement multi-factor authentication and monitor for suspicious activity. Specifically, administrators should configure Microsoft 365 to require users to re-enter their credentials after a device code authorization, and set up alerts for unusual device code authorization requests.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Disclosing new PebbleDash-based tools
items.description: Kaspersky researchers conducted an in-depth analysis of Kimsuky APT activity, revealing tactical shifts and new malware variants based on the PebbleDash platform. The group introduced HelloDoor, a Rust-based backdoor, httpMalice leveraging HTTP and Dropbox communications, and updated MemLoad and httpTroy variants. Kimsuky maintains persistence through legitimate tools including VSCode Tunneling wi
items.ai_summary: Kaspersky researchers discovered new PebbleDash-based tools used by the Kimsuky APT group, including the Rust-based backdoor HelloDoor, httpMalice leveraging HTTP and Dropbox communications, and updated MemLoad and httpTroy variants. These tools enable the group to maintain persistence through legitimate tools like VSCode Tunneling. The introduction of HelloDoor and httpMalice indicates a tactical shift in Kimsuky's tactics, allowing for more sophisticated and adaptable attacks. To remediate, defenders should ensure that all software, including VSCode, is up-to-date, and implement strict access controls to prevent unauthorized use of legitimate tools. Additionally, monitoring for suspicious HTTP and Dropbox communications is crucial to detecting potential Kimsuky activity.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1219| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1550| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1572| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-2868: The Gutenverse – Ultimate WordPress FSE Blocks Addons & Ecosystem plugin for Wor...
items.description: The Gutenverse – Ultimate WordPress FSE Blocks Addons & Ecosystem plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'separatorIconSVG' parameter in versions up to, and including, 3.5.3 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pag
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-2868 is a Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the Gutenverse – Ultimate WordPress FSE Blocks Addons & Ecosystem plugin, affecting versions up to 3.5.3. An authenticated attacker with contributor-level access or above can inject arbitrary web scripts in pages by manipulating the 'separatorIconSVG' parameter. This allows the attacker to steal or forge authentication certificates via T1649, potentially leading to unauthorized access or data tampering. To remediate, update the plugin to version 3.5.4 or later, and ensure that the 'separatorIconSVG' parameter is properly sanitized and escaped. Additionally, consider implementing additional security measures, such as input validation and output encoding, to prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1649| B[Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-1921: The Loco Translate plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Path Traversal in all v...
items.description: The Loco Translate plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Path Traversal in all versions up to, and including, 2.8.2 via the `fsReference` AJAX route. This is due to the `findSourceFile()` method normalizing user-supplied `ref` paths containing `../` directory traversal sequences without validating that the resolved path remains within the intended bundle or content directory. This makes it possibl
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-1921 is a Path Traversal vulnerability in the Loco Translate plugin for WordPress, affecting all versions up to 2.8.2. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by manipulating the `fsReference` AJAX route, leading to unauthorized file access and potential data disclosure. To remediate, update to version 2.8.3 or later, and ensure the `findSourceFile()` method is modified to validate user-supplied `ref` paths. This vulnerability has a CVSS score of 4.9 and is exploitable via the T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) tactic. Defenders should monitor for suspicious AJAX requests and implement web application firewalls to prevent exploitation.

items.title: CVE-2025-13618: The Mentoring plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to privilege escalation in all ...
items.description: The Mentoring plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to privilege escalation in all versions up to, and including, 1.2.8. This is due to the plugin not properly restricting the roles that users can register with in the mentoring_process_registration() function. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to register with administrator-level user accounts.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2025-13618 is a critical privilege escalation vulnerability in the Mentoring plugin for WordPress, affecting all versions up to 1.2.8. The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to register with administrator-level user accounts due to the plugin's failure to properly restrict user roles in the mentoring_process_registration() function. If exploited, this vulnerability enables attackers to gain elevated privileges, potentially leading to data breaches, ransomware attacks, or other malicious activities. To remediate this vulnerability, WordPress administrators should update the Mentoring plugin to version 1.2.9 or later, which addresses the issue. Additionally, it is essential to ensure that all users are properly authenticated and authorized to prevent similar privilege escalation attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-5722: The MoreConvert Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authentication Bypass ...
items.description: The MoreConvert Pro plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authentication Bypass in all versions up to, and including, 1.9.14. This is due to the guest waitlist verification flow not invalidating or regenerating verification tokens when the customer email address is changed. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to authenticate as existing users, including administrators, by obtainin
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-5722 is a critical vulnerability in the MoreConvert Pro plugin for WordPress, affecting all versions up to and including 1.9.14. The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication and authenticate as existing users, including administrators, by obtaining and reusing verification tokens. This can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data and potentially allow attackers to take control of the affected WordPress site. To remediate this vulnerability, WordPress administrators should update the MoreConvert Pro plugin to version 1.9.15 or later, and ensure that all users have strong, unique passwords. Additionally, administrators should consider implementing additional security measures, such as two-factor authentication, to further mitigate the risk of unauthorized access.

items.title: CVE-2026-44029: An issue was discovered in Nix before 2.34.7. Writing to arbitrary files can occ...
items.description: An issue was discovered in Nix before 2.34.7. Writing to arbitrary files can occur via "nix-prefetch-url --unpack" or "nix store prefetch-file --unpack" directory traversal. The fixed versions are 2.34.7, 2.33.6, 2.32.8, 2.31.5, 2.30.5, 2.29.4, and 2.28.7 (introduced in 2.24.7);
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-44029 is a directory traversal vulnerability in Nix before 2.34.7, allowing attackers to write to arbitrary files via 'nix-prefetch-url --unpack' or 'nix store prefetch-file --unpack'. This issue can lead to data breaches or privilege escalation if exploited. To remediate, update to Nix version 2.34.7 or later, specifically 2.33.6, 2.32.8, 2.31.5, 2.30.5, 2.29.4, or 2.28.7. Ensure that 'nix-prefetch-url --unpack' and 'nix store prefetch-file --unpack' are not used with untrusted input. Additionally, consider implementing file system access controls to restrict write access to sensitive directories.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1210| D[Exploitation of Vulnerability]:::tactic
    D -->|T1071| E[File and Directory Discovery]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.description: An issue was discovered in Nix before 2.34.7 and Lix before 2.95.2. Unbounded recursion in the NAR (Nix Archive) parser could lead to a stack-to-heap overflow when the parser is run on a coroutine stack. items.description: On the first day of Pwn2Own Berlin 2026, security researchers collected $523,000 in cash awards after exploiting 24 unique zero-days. [...]

items.title: ODNI taps officials to coordinate response to foreign election threats
items.description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard has tapped two individuals to coordinate work across U.S. spy agencies to monitor threats to the 2026 elections, according to multiple sources familiar with the matter.

items.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
items.description: Cisco has released updates to address a maximum-severity authentication bypass flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Controller that it said has been exploited in limited attacks. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20182, carries a CVSS score of 10.0. "A vulnerability in the peering authentication in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, formerly SD-WAN vSmart, and Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly

items.title: Stealer Backdoor Found in 3 Node-IPC Versions Targeting Developer Secrets
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers are sounding the alarm about what has been described as "malicious activity" in newly published versions of node-ipc. According to Socket and StepSecurity, three different versions of the npm package have been confirmed as malicious - node-ipc@9.1.6 node-ipc@9.2.3 node-ipc@12.0.1 "Early analysis indicates that node-ipc@9.1.6, node-ipc@9.2.3, and node-ipc@12.0.1

items.title: 'FrostyNeighbor' APT Carefully Targets Govt Orgs in Poland, Ukraine
items.description: Attackers uniquely fingerprint victims before delivering spear-phishing payloads aimed at espionage, in the latest campaign from the Belarussian nation-state threat group.

items.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: PAN-OS RCE, Mythos cURL Bug, AI Tokenizer Attacks, and 10+ Stories
items.description: Everything is still on fire. This week feels dumb in the worst way — bad links, weak checks, fake help desks, shady forum posts, and people turning supply chain attacks into some cursed little game for clout and cash. Half of it feels new. Half of it feels like crap we should have fixed years ago. The mess keeps getting louder: users get tricked, boxes get popped, tools meant for normal work

items.title: 18-year-old NGINX vulnerability allows DoS, potential RCE
items.description: An 18-year-old flaw in the NGINX open-source web server, discovered using an autonomous scanning system, can be exploited for denial of service and, under certain conditions, remote code execution. [...]

items.title: Cyber-Enabled Cargo Crime: How Cybercrime Tradecraft is Used to Steal Freight
items.description: Cargo theft now starts with phishing emails and stolen credentials, not hijackings, to reroute and steal freight from supply chains. NMFTA outlines how cyber-enabled cargo crime is changing transportation security. [...]

items.title: Ghostwriter Targets Ukrainian Government With Geofenced PDF Phishing, Cobalt Strike
items.description: The Belarus-aligned threat group known as Ghostwriter has been attributed to a fresh set of attacks targeting governmental organizations in Ukraine. Active since at least 2016, Ghostwriter has been linked to both cyber espionage and influence operations targeting neighboring countries, particularly Ukraine. It's also tracked under the monikers FrostyNeighbor, PUSHCHA, Storm-0257, TA445, UAC‑0057

items.title: Enhancing Data Center Security Without Sacrificing Performance
items.description: For AI data centers, where the stakes are the highest and performance constraints are the tightest, security and performance are no longer a zero-sum game. The post Enhancing Data Center Security Without Sacrificing Performance appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Linux Kernel Vulnerability Fragnesia Allows Root Privilege Escalation
items.description: The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46300, is similar to the recently disclosed exploits named Dirty Frag and Copy Fail. The post New Linux Kernel Vulnerability Fragnesia Allows Root Privilege Escalation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: AI Drives Cybersecurity Investments, Widening 'Valley of Death'
items.description: In a role reversal, investment dollars in security startups exceeded the value of mergers and acquisitions in 1Q26 by more than $1 billion, a rare occurrence.

items.title: Mythos Proves Potent in Vulnerability Discovery, Less Convincing Elsewhere
items.description: Independent benchmarking finds Mythos highly effective for source code audits, reverse engineering, and native-code analysis, though its exploit validation and reasoning capabilities remain inconsistent. The post Mythos Proves Potent in Vulnerability Discovery, Less Convincing Elsewhere appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Akamai to Acquire AI and Browser Security Firm LayerX for $205 Million
items.description: The acquisition enables Akamai to expand its Zero Trust portfolio to add protection directly into the browser. The post Akamai to Acquire AI and Browser Security Firm LayerX for $205 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: KongTuke hackers now use Microsoft Teams for corporate breaches
items.description: Initial access broker KongTuke has moved to Microsoft Teams for social engineering attacks, taking as little as five minutes to gain persistent access to corporate networks. [...]

items.title: Chinese APTs Expand Targets, Update Backdoors in Recent Campaigns
items.description: Salt Typhoon has hit an energy entity in Azerbaijan. Twill Typhoon has targeted Asian entities with an updated RAT. The post Chinese APTs Expand Targets, Update Backdoors in Recent Campaigns appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Foxconn Attack Highlights Manufacturing's Cyber Crisis
items.description: A Nitrogen ransomware attack on Foxconn's North American facilities is one of 600 hits on manufacturers this year, as gangs increasingly target the sector for its low tolerance for downtime.

items.title: PraisonAI CVE-2026-44338 Auth Bypass Targeted Within Hours of Disclosure
items.description: Threat actors have been observed attempting to exploit a recently disclosed security vulnerability in PraisonAI, an open-source multi-agent orchestration framework, within four hours of public disclosure. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-44338 (CVSS score: 7.3), a case of missing authentication that exposes sensitive endpoints to anyone, potentially allowing an attacker to invoke the

items.title: How AI Hallucinations Are Creating Real Security Risks
items.description: AI hallucinations are introducing serious security risks into critical infrastructure decision-making by exploiting human trust through highly confident yet incorrect outputs. When an AI model lacks certainty, it doesn’t have a mechanism to recognize that. Instead, it generates the most probable response based on patterns in its training data, even if that response is inaccurate. These outputs

items.title: G7 Countries Release AI SBOM Guidance
items.description: The goal of the guidance, which outlines minimum elements, is to help organizations enhance transparency in AI systems and supply chains. The post G7 Countries Release AI SBOM Guidance appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: F5 Patches Over 50 Vulnerabilities
items.description: The company’s latest quarterly advisory describes high and medium-severity issues in BIG-IP, BIG-IQ, and NGINX. The post F5 Patches Over 50 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Dell confirms its SupportAssist software causes Windows BSOD crashes
items.description: Dell confirmed that its SupportAssist software is causing blue-screen crashes on some Windows systems following a wave of user reports about random reboots affecting Dell devices since Friday. [...]

items.title: Hackers Targeted PraisonAI Vulnerability Hours After Disclosure
items.description: The first exploitation attempts were observed less than four hours after the authentication bypass was publicly disclosed. The post Hackers Targeted PraisonAI Vulnerability Hours After Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Windows Zero-Days Expose BitLocker Bypasses And CTFMON Privilege Escalation
items.description: An anonymous cybersecurity researcher who disclosed three Microsoft Defender vulnerabilities has returned with two more zero-days involving a BitLocker bypass and a privilege escalation impacting Windows Collaborative Translation Framework (CTFMON). The security defects have been codenamed YellowKey and GreenPlasma, respectively, by the researcher, who goes by the online aliases Chaotic Eclipse

items.title: US charges suspected Dream Market admin arrested in Germany
items.description: The alleged main administrator of Dream Market Incognito Market, one of the largest dark web marketplaces before its shutdown, has been indicted in the United States on money laundering charges. [...]

items.title: High-Severity Vulnerability Patched in VMware Fusion
items.description: The patch was announced as Broadcom is attending the Pwn2Own hacking competition in Berlin this week. The post High-Severity Vulnerability Patched in VMware Fusion appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Researcher Drops YellowKey, GreenPlasma Windows Zero-Days
items.description: YellowKey is a BitLocker bypass that requires physical access. GreenPlasma enables elevation of privileges to System. The post Researcher Drops YellowKey, GreenPlasma Windows Zero-Days appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Fragnesia Linux Kernel LPE Grants Root Access via Page Cache Corruption
items.description: Details have emerged about a new variant of the recent Dirty Frag Linux local privilege escalation (LPE) vulnerability that allows local attackers to gain root access, making it the third such bug to be identified in the kernel within a span of two weeks. Codenamed Fragnesia, the security vulnerability is tracked as CVE-2026-46300 (CVSS score: 7.8) and is rooted in the Linux kernel's XFRM

items.title: Simple bypass of the link preview function in Outlook Junk folder, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: Besides serving as a place where Microsoft Outlook places suspected spam, the Outlook Junk folder has one additional function that can be quite helpful when it comes to identifying malicious messages. Any e-mail placed in this folder is stripped of all formatting, and destinations of all links included in the message become visible to the user, as you can see in the following images which show the same e-mail when it is placed in the inbox, and when it is placed in the Junk folder.

items.title: 18-Year-Old NGINX Rewrite Module Flaw Enables Unauthenticated RCE
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open, including a critical flaw that remained undetected for 18 years. The vulnerability, discovered by depthfirst, is a heap buffer overflow issue impacting ngx_http_rewrite_module (CVE-2026-42945, CVSS v4 score: 9.2) that could allow an attacker to achieve remote code execution or cause a

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, May 14th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9932, (Thu, May 14th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Checkbox Assessments Aren't Fit to Measure Risk
items.description: Security governance needs to be more than an annual compliance exercise. New companies are emerging to address risk-management gaps in current audit tools.

items.title: Attackers Weaponize RubyGems for Data Dead Drops
items.description: Threat actors are publishing RubyGems packages that include scrapers targeting public-facing UK government servers, but with no clear objective.

items.title: Tables Turn on 'The Gentlemen' RaaS Gang With Data Leak
items.description: An OPSEC failure provides a window into what helped the ransomware group rise: a generous affiliate model, opportunistic TTPs, and an effective organizational structure.

items.title: Alleged Dream Market admin arrested in Germany after US indictment
items.description: Court documents said Dream Market was launched in 2013 by Owe Martin Andresen and others before becoming one of the biggest criminal marketplaces online.

items.title: Dark Reading Celebrates 20 Years as a Leading Authority on Cybersecurity, Highlighting the People, Events, Ideas, and Technologies Shaping the Modern Risk Landscape
items.description: Informa TechTarget's flagship cybersecurity media brand launches a special content series to mark two decades as a trusted source for cybersecurity professionals.

items.title: European Commission head pushes creation of new law delaying teens’ social media access
items.description: The comments come as several European countries, including Spain, Greece, Norway, France, Denmark, Turkey and the Netherlands have said they are considering or are implementing age verification protocols to restrict young teens from accessing social media platforms.

items.title: Microsoft's MDASH AI System Finds 16 Windows Flaws Fixed in Patch Tuesday
items.description: Microsoft has unveiled a new multi-model artificial intelligence (AI)-driven system called MDASH to facilitate vulnerability discovery and remediation at scale, adding that it's being tested by some customers as part of a limited private preview. MDASH, short for multi-model agentic scanning harness, is designed as a model-agnostic system that uses bespoke AI agents for different vulnerability

items.title: China's 'FamousSparrow' APT Nests in South Caucasus Energy Firm
items.description: The cyberthreat group targets an Azerbaijani oil and gas firm with repeated attacks, as the China-linked actors extend targeting beyond hospitality, telecom, and government sectors.

items.title: UK moves to shield security researchers in cybercrime law overhaul
items.description: The proposed reforms, outlined in briefing documents published alongside the King’s Speech opening a new parliamentary session, would update the Computer Misuse Act 1990 as part of a broader national security package focused on cybercrime and digital threats.

items.title: [GUEST DIARY] Tearing apart website fraud to see how it works., (Wed, May 13th)
items.description: &&#x23;x26;&#x23;x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Joshua Nikolson, an ISC Intern and part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program.]

items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Proxying the Unproxyable? Sending EXE traffic to a Proxy, (Wed, May 13th)
items.description: .. if â€œunproxyableâ€ is a word that is ..

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Microsoft May 2026 Patch Tuesday, (Tue, May 12th)
items.description: Today&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Microsoft patch Tuesday fixes 137 different vulnerabilities. In addition, the update addresses 137 Chromium-related issues affecting Microsoft Edge.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, May 12th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9928, (Tue, May 12th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Apple Patches Everything, (Mon, May 11th)
items.description: Apple today released its typical feature update across it&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s operating systems (iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, vision OS). With this update, Apple patched 84 different vulnerabilities. Updates are available for the "26" series of operating systems, as well as for the previous "18" version of iOS/iPadOS, and two versions back for macOS (version 14 and 15).

items.title: Why we use CAPTCHAs, (Mon, May 11th)
items.description: A few months ago, I implemented Cloudflare&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;s Turnstile CAPTCHA on some pages. The reason for implementing these CAPTCHAs is obvious: Bots make up a large percentage of traffic and affect site performance.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, May 11th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9926, (Mon, May 11th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
